title: erigeron acris var. elatus
intro: Erigeron acris var. elatus, also known as tall fleabane, is a plant species within the daisy family that is native to mountainous regions in Europe and Asia. The medicinal properties of Erigeron acris var. elatus lie in its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and antitussive properties. Erigeron acris var. elatus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a versatile remedy in traditional medicine.
